title: Roblox: Strong Growth Clouded by Compensation Concerns
text: Roblox delivered robust fourth-quarter results with bookings surging 63% year-over-year to $2.22 billion. The company achieved revenue growth of 43% to reach $1.42 billion. Key user metrics also improved significantly, with daily active users climbing 69% to 144 million and monthly unique payers jumping 94% to 36.7 million. However, a troubling metric undermines profitability. Stock-based compensation expense is massive, and when taking that into consideration, the company is nowhere close to becoming profitable. In the quarter alone, Roblox recorded $298.4 million in stock-based compensation expenses, totaling $1.13 billion for the full year. This compensation strategy significantly impacts adjusted EBITDA, which fell from $65.6 million to just $2.7 million despite strong revenue expansion. Management provided optimistic guidance, projecting revenue growth of 23-29% and bookings growth of 22-26% for the upcoming year. However, the substantial stock-based compensation burden prevents meaningful profitability, warranting caution for potential investors.
